data.title: Net power outlines $1.7B-$2B cost target for SN1 clean gas power plant amid optimization efforts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NET Power Inc. (NPWR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Danny Rice highlighted significant progress for 2024, including completing the front-end engineering and design (FEED) for Project Permian (SN1), the world's first utility-scale clean gas power plant. The company also initiated its equipment validation program with Baker Hughes at the La Porte facility, achieving over 140 fired hours during testing.
  • Management discussed inflationary pressures impacting cost estimates, revising the total installed cost for SN1 to $1.7 billion to $2 billion. The company is now focusing on post-FEED optimization to reduce costs while maintaining performance.
  • CEO Rice emphasized the strategic position of Net Power to address growing energy demand, particularly from AI and data centers. He stated that "Net Power can be the most logical solution to deliver clean, reliable and affordable energy."
  • CFO Akash Patel reported the company ended 2024 with $533 million in liquidity. He detailed operational cash outflows of $13 million and capital expenditures of $29 million for Q4, primarily for La Porte upgrades and SN1 development.

Outlook

  • Management outlined priorities for 2025, which include completing the value engineering process for SN1, advancing feasibility studies for Gulf Coast multi-unit projects, and securing capital for commercialization efforts.
  • CEO Rice stated that groundbreaking for SN1 is targeted for 2027, with an in-service date in 2029, contingent on securing necessary funding.
  • The company plans to focus on modular multi-unit deployments along the Gulf Coast to enhance scalability and cost efficiency. Management also signaled interest in pursuing industrial-scale applications through a collaboration with Baker Hughes and Woodside Energy.

Financial Results

  • CFO Patel highlighted operating cash outflows of $32 million and capital expenditures of $70 million for 2024. The company allocated $200 million of its liquidity for SN1 and has spent $50 million to date on engineering and long-lead items.
  • Patel noted that current SN1 economics support approximately $600 million in project-level financing, leaving a funding gap of $600 million to $900 million to fully fund the project.
  • Management is exploring multiple avenues to address the funding gap, including project-level capital, strategic partnerships, and potential government support.

Q&A

  • Analyst Thomas Meric, Janney Montgomery Scott: Questioned the breakdown of labor and material costs in the FEED estimate. COO Brian Allen explained that cost increases stem from market supply-demand imbalances, first-of-a-kind project specificities, and site-specific challenges like inland transport and nitrogen content in natural gas.
  • Analyst Martin Malloy, Johnson Rice: Inquired about modularization milestones and timelines. COO Allen confirmed work is underway to maximize modularization for coastal locations and reduce costs through pre-fabrication and multi-unit configurations.
  • Analyst Noel Parks, Tuohy Brothers: Raised concerns about power demand pressures driven by AI. CEO Rice noted that tight supply chains and rising grid demand reinforce the need for scalable, reliable solutions like Net Power's technology.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about the scalability and funding of SN1 amid rising costs, reflecting a slightly negative sentiment.
  • Management's tone during prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing the long-term potential of their technology. However, responses to analyst questions were more defensive, particularly regarding cost overruns and funding gaps.
  • Compared to Q3, management's tone shifted to acknowledge challenges while reiterating their strategic focus on cost optimization and market positioning.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for SN1 costs increased from $1.1 billion to a range of $1.7 billion to $2 billion due to inflationary pressures and site-specific challenges.
  • In Q3, management emphasized technical progress at La Porte and early-stage project development. In Q4, the focus shifted to funding challenges and cost optimization efforts.
  • Q&A discussions revealed heightened analyst scrutiny on cost escalations and funding paths compared to the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Inflationary pressures and supply chain constraints remain significant challenges, impacting SN1 cost estimates and project timelines.
  • The company faces a funding gap for SN1 of $600 million to $900 million, with uncertainties around securing additional capital.
  • Project-specific challenges, such as inland transport and nitrogen purification, contribute to higher costs and complexity.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the scalability and competitiveness of Net Power's technology amid rising costs.

Final Takeaway

Net Power is advancing its innovative clean gas power technology but faces significant cost and funding challenges. The company remains focused on cost optimization, strategic partnerships, and modular deployment strategies to address escalating costs and market demand for reliable, clean energy solutions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Johnson and Johnson will not exercise worldwide license option for HexaBody-CD38, says Genmab
data.description:
Genmab A/S (Nasdaq: GMAB) said on Monday that Johnson & Johnson (J&J) has decided not to exercise its option to obtain a worldwide license for the development, manufacturing, and commercialization of HexaBody-CD38.

Despite initial clinical data showing efficacy, Genmab has decided to discontinue further clinical development of HexaBody-CD38.

“While we are disappointed that J&J has decided not to advance HexaBody-CD38, the data confirms the clinical potential of the HexaBody platform, reinforcing its value for future applications,” said Jan van de Winkel, Ph.D., Chief Executive Officer of Genmab.

Preliminary trial results submitted to J&J included data which demonstrated an overall response rate of 55%. However, due to the short follow-up period, key secondary measures such as duration of response, progression-free survival, and overall survival were not yet fully assessed.

Genmab  confirmed that this development will not impact its 2025 financial guidance.

GMAB shares are down about 7%.

data.title: S&P 500 is on pace to close below its 200-day MA for the first time in 16 months
data.description:
The recent sell-off on Wall Street has caused the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) to drop below its 200-day moving average, putting the index on track to close beneath this technical level for the first time in over 16 months.

The last time the S&P 500 closed below its 200-day moving average was back on November 1, 2023, well over a year ago.

In addition to this technical breakdown, the index also has experienced a 2.1% decline on Monday, pushing it closer to a six-month trading low. During the session, the S&P 500 briefly reached 5,638.84, its lowest point since September 18, 2023. 

Selling pressures have intensified as concerns surrounding persistent tariff issues continue to weigh heavily on market sentiment. Additionally, the growing discourse around the potential for an economic recession has added to the uncertainty, amplifying fears among investors. This combination of factors has created a volatile environment as seen in the VIX (VIX) which has topped highs not seen since December 18 with many now questioning the sustainability of economic growth amid these ongoing challenges.

For investors looking to track the S&P 500’s movements, there are several index ETFs and mutual funds worth considering. Some options include:

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Draganfly opens new U.S. facility in Tampa with focus on national security, defense partnerships
data.description:
• Draganfly (NASDAQ:DPRO) Monday announced the establishment of its new U.S. location in Tampa, Florida.
  • Positioned near key military and government clients, this expansion includes a demonstration and live fire testing facility.
  • The Tampa facility is part of Draganfly’s long-term strategy to grow its government business, aligning with increasing demand from defense, law enforcement, and industrial sectors.

  • Shares -8.75% to $2.19.

data.title: Mineralys surges 53% on pivotal data for hypertension asset lorundrostat
data.description:
• Mineralys Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MLYS) is up ~53% in Monday morning trading after reporting positive topline results from a pair of pivotal trials on lorundrostat for uncontrolled or resistant hypertension.
  • The two studies were the phase 3 Launch-HTN and phase 2 Advance-HTN.
  • Launch-HTN demonstrated that patients on lorundrostat achieved statistically significant mean reduction from baseline in placebo-adjusted systolic blood pressure at week six with potential future reduction through week 12.
  • The other trial, which is examining lorundrostat as an add-on therapy, achieved its primary endpoint: placebo-adjusted reduction from baseline in systolic blood pressure.
  • Lorundrostat is an oral, highly selective aldosterone synthase inhibitor.

data.title: Major U.S. airlines get slammed as recession fears add to woes
data.description:
Airline stocks are nosediving in Monday's open market trading. Major U.S. carriers American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL), Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV), Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL), Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK), and United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) are on track for a three-day losing streak.

U.S. equities are in red as Wall Street opened sharply lower on growing concerns about the economy's health after a Donald Trump interview with Fox News stoked fear among investors. The tech-heavy Nasdaq (COMP:IND) fell 3%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is down 1.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is lower by 0.8%.

When asked about a potential recession in the U.S., Trump said, “I hate to predict things like that. There is a period of transition because what we’re doing is very big. We’re bringing wealth back to America. That’s a big thing.”

Separately, flying sentiment has been low since mid-February following a Delta (NYSE:DAL) plane crash in Toronto that injured at least 15 people, three of them critically. The incident involved a flight from Minneapolis that landed at Pearson International Airport.

The U.S. also has a shortage of experienced air traffic controllers.  A New York Times analysis from January suggested more than 90% of the 313 air traffic control facilities in the country are operating below the FAA’s recommended staffing levels. There are currently about 10,800 air traffic controllers, 3,600 fewer than "what is needed to fully staff the National Airspace System," according to the National Air Traffic Controllers Association.

The U.S. Global JETS ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS) is down nearly 13% so far this year and is currently off 17% from its January high.

Shares of American (NASDAQ:AAL), United (NASDAQ:UAL), Delta (DAL), Southwest (NYSE:LUV), and Alaska (NYSE:ALK) are down between 3% and 8%, while discount carriers Sun Country (NASDAQ:SNCY), Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC), and JetBlue Airways (NASDAQ:JBLU) are down between 6% and 8%. Regional airline SkyWest (NASDAQ:SKYW) is down nearly 7%.

data.title: VIX surge above 25 implies above-average ‘but far from perilous’ recession risk
data.description:
Societe Generale said Monday the surge in Wall Street’s volatility gauge (VIX) above 25 suggests a heightened, but not hazardous, risk of an economic recession. 

The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) shot up to its highest since September 2022 as the S&P 500 (SP500)(NYSEARCA:VOO)(NYSEARCA:IVV) sank ~2% Monday. The moves came after U.S. President Donald Trump did not stomp out recession concerns in a weekend Fox News interview. The (VIX) surged +14% to 26.73 in late-morning trade. 

“The current level of 25+ VIX suggests around 19% probability - which is c.1.8x long-term average of 11%. In other words, equity volatility implies a higher-than-normal probability of recession,” Jitesh Kumar, derivatives strategist at Societe Generale, said in a note. “Above average, but far from perilous,” SocGen said. 

Kumar also said other economic data and financial assets currently suggest a low probability of recession.

"The manufacturing sector and short-term rates are the only two series signalling higher risk of recession - not surprising given manufacturing new orders dipped below 50 in the most recent data release,” he said, and published this chart: 

 

 

 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dropped 3% during Monday trade, and the Dow (DJI) was off by 1%. Stocks finished lower last week.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: AppLovin shares continue to fall as Citi reiterates Buy rating
data.description:
AppLovin's (NASDAQ:APP) recent steep slide continues, with shares down 11% during early market action on Monday.

After reaching a record high of $510 on February 14, share value has retreated more than 50% since. Before this recent plunge, AppLovin shares had increased several times over in the past year.

However, despite the setback, Citi Research maintains its Buy rating and lofty $600 price target on the digital advertising and e-commerce company.

Citi contends the recent plunge is related to a "series of bearish reports that, we believe, make spurious claims about AppLovin and a broader sell-off in momentum stocks."

"Based on peer's revenue growth rates, EBITDA margins and equity values, AppLovin should be worth $550 a share," said Citi analysts, led by Jason Bazinet, in an investor note. "The prevailing equity value (of $260) suggests the market is ascribing a ~50% likelihood that AppLovin's equity is worth $0. That strikes us as remarkably high. We suspect this has less to do with the merits of the bears claims. More likely, in our view, it stems from AppLovin's rapid success, opaque business model and investor's long-held skepticism about AdTech business models."

Seeking Alpha analyst Richard Durant wrote last week that AppLovin's valuation is elevated, and it will be difficult for the stock to continue providing strong results.

"I tend to think that the stock is probably reasonably valued at the moment, although there could still be significant upside if AppLovin's dominance can be extended beyond gaming and into areas like CTV," Durant noted. "Given some of the uncertainty surrounding AppLovin's business and the broader mobile gaming industry, I think it is reasonable to question the merits of paying such a high revenue multiple, despite AppLovin's amazing financials."

data.title: Summit Midstream to buy Moonrise Midstream in $90M cash and stock deal
data.description:
Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) -3.2% in Monday's trading after saying it agreed to acquire Moonrise Midstream from Fundare Resources for $90M, including $70M in cash and $20M in shares, continuing its strategy of building scale through strategic, bolt-on acquisitions in key basins.

Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) said the deal expands its gathering and processing footprint in the DJ Basin with ~80 miles of natural gas gathering pipeline, 25 miles of crude oil gathering pipeline and 65M cf/day of additional processing capacity, expandable to ~100M cf/day.

The company said it is already operationally connected to the Moonrise system through multiple existing interconnections, and it expects to capture significant operational and commercial synergies with the combined systems.

"This acquisition not only expands our footprint and dedicated acreage in one of the most active areas of the DJ Basin but also provides incremental processing capacity that we expect to utilize in the coming years as volumes behind our legacy systems continue to grow," Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) Chairman, President and CEO Heath Deneke said.

data.title: Camden Property, IRT, MAA raised to Buy on lower Sunbelt supply, rent prospects
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Linda Tsai on Monday upgraded apartment REITs Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT), Independence Realty Trust (NYSE:IRT) and Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA), each to Buy from Hold, as supply in the Sunbelt region is set to improve from heightened levels, potentially driving a better growth outlook for the REITs.

"Heightened supply, the key headwind facing Sunbelt REITs (CPT, IRT, MAA) is coming down," Tsai wrote in a note to clients, citing a moderation in CoStar's estimates for future supply in Q1 2025 vs. Q4 2024. 

"This is consistent with the three REITs' 4Q24 earnings commentary - all point to moderating supply & improved confidence in 2H25 leasing spreads," she added.

The analyst also pointed to what she believes is conservative guidance seen across the three companies. "CPT, IRT, and MAA each have a history of guiding conservatively and beating their initial guidance, and 2025 should play out similarly."

The REITs traded higher in Monday morning trading, with (NYSE:CPT) +2.4%, (NYSE:IRT) +2.8% and (NYSE:MAA) +2.7%.

Seeking Alpha's peer comparison page assesses various metrics for the three stocks.
